title: Blacklist grace period leaves a window where a rotated token still validates
body: Blacklist.php's default `$gracePeriod` of 0 seconds should mean an old token is invalid immediately after refresh, but under concurrent requests we've seen the just-refreshed old token accepted once more within the same second it was blacklisted. Is there an inherent race between the blacklist write and a concurrent request's read, independent of the grace period setting?

title: No validation that JWT_SECRET is set before signing tokens
body: config/config.php pulls the signing secret straight from `env('JWT_SECRET')` with no default and no runtime assertion. If the env var is missing in a given environment (e.g. a misconfigured staging deploy), tokens appear to get signed with a null/empty secret instead of failing loudly at boot.

title: RefreshToken middleware reissues a token on read-only GET requests
body: The RefreshToken middleware issues a brand new token + sets it on every request it wraps, including plain GET requests that don't need a longer-lived session yet. This adds unnecessary Authorization header churn for read-heavy API clients. Could refresh be limited to requests past a configurable staleness threshold?

title: Lcobucci provider doesn't expose leeway configuration from lcobucci/jwt v5
body: Upgrading our app's lcobucci/jwt dependency to v5, we noticed src/Providers/JWT/Lcobucci.php doesn't have a way to configure clock leeway for `exp`/`nbf` validation like the newer lcobucci API supports. Small clock drift between app servers is now causing occasional spurious TokenExpiredException.

title: Unclear how JWTGuard behaves alongside a stateful Sanctum guard
body: We're migrating an app that has both `jwt` and Sanctum's stateful session guard registered for different route groups. JWTGuard.php doesn't document what happens if a request carries both a valid session cookie and a JWT bearer token — which one does `Auth::user()` resolve through when both guards are attached to the same request pipeline?
